pattern: Simulate the sequential insertions into an initially empty list using the built-in insert operation.
hints: Process nums and index in tandem: at step i, insert nums[i] at position index[i] in the growing result list.
Python's list.insert(pos, val) does exactly this in one line per element.
Insertion at position p shifts all elements from p onward one position to the right.

edge_inputs.description: all inserted at position 0, reverse order
max length 10 all inserted at 0, stress covers larger sizes
insertion in the middle shifts subsequent elements
example 2: last insert at position 0 puts it first

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(0, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    index = [rng.randint(0, i) for i in range(n)]
    return [nums, index]
